summary: PR 2170 linked `sysdolphin/baselib/gobjinit.c` by changing its build status in `configure.py` from `NonMatching` to `Matching`. This was a minimal build-configuration update indicating that `gobjinit.c` had become matching and could now be included as a matched object in the configured build.

key_files.reason: Changed the object registration for `sysdolphin/baselib/gobjinit.c` from `Object(NonMatching, ...)` to `Object(Matching, ...)` inside the `MatchingFor` object list.

what_changed: The neighboring baselib GObj files in the object list include `gobjobject.c`, `gobjuserdata.c`, and `gobj.c`, which were already marked `Matching` in the shown diff context.
No source code changes, comments, or review discussion were present in the provided PR slice.
smart_moves: Kept the PR narrowly scoped to the one build-system state change required to link `gobjinit.c` as matching.
Used the existing `Object(Matching, "path")` convention in `configure.py`, avoiding unnecessary build-system churn.
decomp_lessons: Once a decompiled file is verified matching, update its `configure.py` object status from `NonMatching` to `Matching` so the matched object is linked by the standard build.
Small matching-status PRs are useful for isolating link-state changes from the source-level decompilation work that made the file match.
For sysdolphin baselib work, keep related GObj files consistently classified in the build object list as they become matching.
naming_conventions: Build configuration uses `Object(Matching, "sysdolphin/baselib/gobjinit.c")` for matched source files.
Non-matching source files are tracked with `Object(NonMatching, "...")` in the same object list.
The file path follows the sysdolphin baselib GObj naming pattern: `sysdolphin/baselib/gobj*.c`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly-level matching tactics are documented in the PR text or diff excerpt.
The only evidenced matching action is flipping the object classification from `NonMatching` to `Matching` after the file had apparently reached matching status.
follow_up_queries: Find the PR or commit that made `sysdolphin/baselib/gobjinit.c` source match before this link-status flip.
Search for remaining `sysdolphin/baselib/gobj*.c` files still marked `NonMatching` in `configure.py`.
Check whether later PRs changed neighboring baselib files such as `particle.c`, `psdisp.c`, or `psdisptev.c` from `NonMatching` to `Matching`.
